[TITLE]: Nicole Kidman Finally Addresses Why She Changes Her Hair So Much - AOL
[METADATA/INTRO]: Nicole Kidman Finally Addresses Why She Changes Her Hair So Much Nicole Kidman recently explained why she's always sporting new hairstyles: she loves changing her hair....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “Everyone’s like, ‘Did you chop your hair off?’ I’m like, ‘No, I did not,'” she said, seemingly confirming it was a wig. As for her reasoning? It's quite simple: "I love changing my hair." 
 Following Kidman's Met Gala look, her most recent appearance was at this year's Cannes Film Festival, where she once again wore a wig. This time, however, her hair of choice was long and strawberry blonde, much like her natural color. It was the Holland actress's first time at the festival in eight years. 
 Kidman stepped out for two related events: the Kering Women in Motion Talk, where she spoke, and the Kering Women in Motion Awards. She received an award at the latter event for her advocacy in the film industry.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016-02-05
[TITLE]: Nicole Kidman: five best moments | Nicole Kidman | The Guardian
[METADATA/INTRO]: It’s set to be a banner year for Nicole Kidman. Not only is she involved in a number of advertising campaigns (Airline! Vitamins! Meerkats!) but the Oscar-winning actor is also appearing in a number of interesting projects for both the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: It’s set to be a banner year for Nicole Kidman. Not only is she involved in a number of advertising campaigns (Airline! Vitamins! Meerkats!) but the Oscar-winning actor is also appearing in a number of interesting projects for both the big and small screen. 
 This month’s Berlin film festival will see the premiere of Genius, a biopic of the literary editor Max Perkins that Kidman stars in alongside Colin Firth, Jude Law and Laura Linney. Her turns in festival premieres from last year, Werner Herzog’s Queen of the Desert and Jason Bateman’s The Family Fang, will also get released. Plus she will pop up in John Cameron Mitchell’s sci-fi comedy How to Talk to Girls at Parties, the Oscar-tipped drama Lion and the HBO series Big Little Lies, with Reese Witherspoon.
